workflow_name: Customer Escalation Email -> Risk Brief + Manager Loop
user_message: Just got this from Karen Patel at VanArsdel Ltd -- I need help figuring out security posture. Not urgent but would be good to have by EOD.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Karen Patel <karen.patel@vanarsdelltd.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-23 17:26 EST
Subject: Follow-up: security posture Review

Just checking in on the security posture proposal. Our CTO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Karen Patel <karen.patel@vanarsdelltd.com>
Date: 2024-02-10 09:58 EST
Subject: URGENT: security posture Issue at VanArsdel Ltd

Just checking in on the security posture proposal. Our CTO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Karen Patel <karen.patel@vanarsdelltd.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-03 16:45 EST
Subject: URGENT: security posture Issue at VanArsdel Ltd

Our team encountered some challenges with security posture. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF34-M trap (Strict separation of manager brief from customer draft when one unified response); produces complete Severity assessment, manager risk brief, customer-facing draft response, stakeholder map delta, fore
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF34-M failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF34-M failure mode (Strict separation of manager brief from customer draft when one unified response)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct security posture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF34-M -- Strict separation of manager brief from customer draft when one unified response
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
